anti_claim: The macro projection import protocol validates public-safe projection metadata, omission receipts, replacement refs, authority ceilings, and validation refs. It does not copy private bodies, authorize release or publication, claim private-root equivalence, elevate Microcosm above its public receipts, run provider calls, or make macro source authority public.

findings.message: Projection import cannot upgrade public metadata into live macro source authority.

Projection import rejects omitted material without omission receipts.
Projection import rejects cells without validation refs.
Projection import rejects private body import requests.
Projection import rejects release, publication, recipient, and private-equivalence claims.
Standalone release rejects runtime dependencies on macro/private roots.

acceptance_questions: What real macro-origin bodies, tools, and patterns are now visible?
What can a cold reader run locally from the Microcosm tree?
Which refs are provenance-only rather than runtime dependencies?
Which validation receipts prove the allowed flow and the blocked true-private flow?
Which release, publication, and private-equivalence claims remain false?

cold_reader_answers.provenance_surface: source refs, body material ids, validation refs, and standalone severance board

lanes.claim_ceiling: machine-evidence-routed proof/readiness specimen only; no whole-theorem or whole-system correctness claim
lanes.why_external_reader_cares: Shows that Lean/proof material is one normal public-safe body lane, tied to verifier traces and evidence cells instead of isolated spectacle.

lanes.claim_ceiling: public readiness and evaluator lens only; no provider success, proof success, or benchmark performance claim
lanes.why_external_reader_cares: Turns macro prover machinery into inspectable local readiness, strategy, and routing artifacts.

lanes.claim_ceiling: governed public replay and receipt closure only; no live repo mutation or publication claim
lanes.why_external_reader_cares: Makes the operational spine visible: dirty trees, claims, scoped commits, receipts, and closure are product behavior, not hidden process.

lanes.claim_ceiling: public route/option surface only; no guarantee that every future agent route is correct
lanes.why_external_reader_cares: Shows the substrate's distinctive route intelligence: agents get bounded options, route leases, and salience rather than blind grep.

lanes.claim_ceiling: public compiler/read-model substrate only; no claim that every macro pattern is fully imported
lanes.why_external_reader_cares: Turns the 374-row macro pattern ledger from planning context into visible product substrate.

lanes.claim_ceiling: observable standalone product surface only; no private-root equivalence or hosted-public release claim
lanes.why_external_reader_cares: Makes the product perceivable: what works, what is only provenance, and which release-tree refs are safe to run.

dependency_preflight_gate.anti_claim: This gate proves release severance consumed the public preflight lifecycle receipt. It does not authorize release, publication, provider calls, private-source equivalence, or proof of organ semantics.
